symptoms.symptom_name: Deep constant central abdominal pain radiating to the lower back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New non-mechanical central abdominal/back pain in an older hypertensive is a cardinal symptomatic-AAA feature.
Back pain is also a dissection feature; both are aortic emergencies.
Back pain is non-specific but the non-mechanical constant character argues against a mechanical cause.
A felt pulsation corresponds to an expansile aortic mass — a high-value red flag when combined with pain.
Postural lightheadedness suggests early hypovolaemia from aneurysm expansion or contained leak.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pulsatile expansile abdominal mass on palpation (supra-umbilical)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pulsatile expansile mass with pain is near-diagnostic — but requires in-person palpation and is sealed as a clinician finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain.
Turbulent flow over the dilated aorta; a clinician auscultation finding, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor with borderline hypotension (BP 105/65) and tachycardia (HR 98)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensated haemorrhagic shock physiology; the measured vitals are a sealed clinician measurement, though pallor is caregiver-observable.
Hypertension is a major risk factor for aneurysm formation, expansion and rupture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain unaffected by movement or position (pertinent negative for mechanical cause)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical back pain typically varies with movement/position; a constant non-mechanical pain argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No costovertebral-angle tenderness; no urinary symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of loin tenderness and urinary features makes renal colic less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no left-lower-quadrant localisation, no altered bowel habit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diverticulitis usually gives fever, LLQ peritonism and bowel change — all absent.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT angiography of the aorta showing aneurysmal dilatation and rupture status

investigation_details.expected_result: Infrarenal aortic diameter >3 cm with features of expansion or contained/frank rupture
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without emergency imaging, aneurysm size and rupture status are unknown and definitive surgical planning is impossible.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT angiography is the confirmatory and operative-planning investigation; unavailable in telehealth and performed only after emergency transfer.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Deep central/back pain + a felt abdominal pulsation + presyncope in an older hypertensive is a high-probability symptomatic-AAA cluster mandating emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the aneurysm, measures diameter and defines rupture status for emergency vascular surgery — performed after immediate transfer, not remotely.
Rapid confirmation of aortic dilatation where available on arrival; not obtainable via telehealth chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC, group and hold / crossmatch, coagulation profile, renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Pre-operative and resuscitation bloods for a potential ruptured aneurysm — hospital-based.
Deferring imaging to an outpatient pathway is unsafe for a symptomatic aneurysm — this is an emergency requiring immediate transfer.
